title: Methods named Dispose should belong to a disposable type
messageFormat: Rename this method or implement IDisposable; 'Dispose' is reserved for the disposable pattern.
summary: The name Dispose is a strong signal that a type participates in the IDisposable pattern and that callers, including using statements, can release it deterministically. Putting a Dispose method on a type that does not implement IDisposable breaks that expectation: the method will not be called by using or by disposal-aware containers. Either implement the interface so the method is the real disposal entry point, or give the method a name that describes what it actually does.
